title: Overnight Backup Job Failure Due to Expired Credentials
symptoms: Backup job in FAILED status in AWS Backup console
Backup service account authentication failure in logs
Backup vault showing no new recovery points since previous day
Automated backup verification email not received by ops team
Service account password expiry notification sent 14 days prior but unactioned
root_cause: Backup service account credentials expired after 90-day rotation policy, and the password rotation notification was not actioned by the team, leaving the backup job unable to authenticate
resolution: Rotated the backup service account credentials, updated the backup job configuration, executed a manual backup to restore the recovery point, and set up credential expiry monitoring
preventive_actions: Implement service account credential rotation automation
Add backup job success/failure monitoring with immediate alerts
Use IAM roles instead of service account credentials where possible
Create service account credential expiry dashboard
Implement backup verification automation with immediate escalation on failure

timeline.event: Overnight backup job starts and immediately fails with auth error
Backup job logs authentication failure, no retry configured
Morning backup verification check reveals missing backup
Chris Evans investigates, identifies credential expiry
Service account credentials rotated and updated
Manual backup initiated to restore recovery point
Backup monitoring alerts configured, incident resolved

embedding_summary: Overnight database backup job failed due to expired service account credentials from 90-day password rotation policy. Failure undetected for 6 hours. RPO extended to 48 hours. Resolved by rotating credentials, executing manual backup, and implementing credential expiry automation.
